zh.lifeSummary: Hans I. Schwartz於大約1500年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hans I. Schwartz于大约1500年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Schwartz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Emanuel, Meyer, Chaim, Isadore, Moshe, Aron, Ari, Mayer, Hershel.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for someone with black hair or a dark complexion, from Middle High German swarz, German schwarz, Yiddish shvarts ‘dark, black’. In some cases this may be a habitational name from any of the places called Schwarz, named with the same word. This form of the surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Denmark, Sweden, Hungary, etc. Compare Schwarcz , Schwarz , Shwartz , Swarts , and Swartz .

variants.name: Schwartze
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Schwartz  .

variants.name: Schwarze
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Kurt, Erhard, Helmut, Hermann, Joerg, Jurgen, Reiner, Wilfried.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Schwartz  .

variants.name: Schwarting
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Fritz.
Dutch and North German: patronymic from a nickname from Middle Low German schwart ‘black’. Compare German Schwartz .

variants.name: Swartz
meanings: Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schwartz or Schwarz ‘dark, black’, and of their Slavicized forms Švarc (Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian) and Szwarc (Polish).
Dutch: variant of   Swarts  .

variants.name: Shvarts
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Vladimir, Yury, Anatoly, Betya, Grigoriy, Igor, Iosif, Leonid, Lev, Mikhail, Yefim.  Jewish Yakov, Alter, Ber, Golda, Itsik, Mikhael, Moisey, Sarra.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant, transliterated from Russian, of   Schwartz  .

variants.name: Schwarcz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Zalmon, Dov, Moises, Moishe, Zev.  Hungarian Jeno, Laszlo, Sandor, Zoltan.
Hungarian and Jewish (from Hungary): variant of   Schwartz  .

variants.name: Schwarte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Heribert, Horst.
North German and Dutch: nickname for someone with black hair or a dark complexion. Compare German   Schwartz  .

variants.name: Schwarz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gunter, Heinz, Wolfgang, Gerhard, Manfred, Klaus, Bernhard, Ernst.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Schwartz . This surname is also found in Slovakia, Czechia, Poland, Croatia, and Slovenia, where it is more common in Slavicized forms (see 2 below) and where it was often applied as a translation into German of Slavic cognates like Czech and Slovak Černý (see Cerny 1), Slovenian Černe (see Cerne 1). It is also found in some western European countries, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and the Netherlands.
Germanized form of Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian Švarc (see Swartz 2) and Polish Szwarc , surnames of German origin (see above).

variants.name: Shwartz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Eliyahu, Meyer, Tali.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Schwartz   or   Schwarz   ‘dark, black’.

nameMeanings.name: Hans
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Johannes .

variants.name: Hansel
meanings: Pet form of   Johannes .
